# SC-04: Data Classification & Sensitivity Screening

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** SHOULD (recommended) · **Status:** draft

At intent ingestion, classify all data types referenced in the change request: Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted (includes PII), or Regulated (financial, health, biometric). This classification flows downstream and gates encryption-at-rest and in-transit requirements, access control stringency, retention policy, audit logging depth, and applicable regulatory regimes (GDPR, DORA, AI Act). Requires data owner or DPO review before proceeding.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 1 | Intent Ingestion | Mandatory | *Classification must be determined before design begins. It informs all downstream security and governance decisions. Classification persists in GC-02 traceability.*

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Agent classifies, human validates |
| **Agent does** | Analyse the feature specification and identify all data types: user data, transactional data, AI training data, logs, etc. Propose a sensitivity classification for each type (Public/Internal/Confidential/Restricted/Regulated). Flag any data types that trigger GDPR, DORA Art. 9, or EU AI Act Art. 10 obligations. |
| **Human does** | Review agent-proposed classifications. Confirm or override with documented justification. Engage Data Protection Officer (DPO) or data owner for Restricted or Regulated data. Sign off on final classification before design begins. |

## Inputs

| Artifact | Source Control | Description |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| Feature specification | QC-01 | Description of intended functionality and data flows |
| Data dictionary or schema | Technical specification of data structures and fields |  |


## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Data classification inventory | decision | Mapping of each data type to sensitivity level (Public/Internal/Confidential/Restricted/Regulated). For each Restricted or Regulated classification, document: legal basis for processing, retention policy, access controls required, encryption requirements. | 7 years (audit trail) |
| DPO or data owner sign-off | decision | Documented approval from data steward for Restricted/Regulated data | 7 years |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_change |
| **SLA** | 60 min |
| **Tooling** | Data classification taxonomies (internal or NIST), GDPR impact assessment templates |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Data Protection Officer |

If data classification cannot be determined or agreed upon, escalate to DPO. Default to highest applicable sensitivity level pending resolution.

## Dependencies

- `QC-01`


## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 9(2) | High standards for data protection across all states (at rest, in transit, in use); calibration depends on knowing data sensitivity |
| DORA | Art. 9(4) | Cryptographic key management "based on data classification" is explicit requirement; presupposes that classification exists |
| EU AI Act | Art. 10 | Data governance and management must examine possible biases and risks to fundamental rights; requires understanding what data is being processed |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security, Data Governance · **Owner role:** Data Governance Lead
